item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
The first image could not be sourced reliably and was taken by a photographer on a helicopter, presumably on a research expedition. It is assumed not compliant with CC 3.0.

The second image is an image of tacca chantrieri. The exact image could not be sourced reliably and is assumed not compliant with CC 3.0.

As both images are assumed not compliant, they have been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This Scip has problems, but the line:

These entities will take the form of anything the observer considers to be valuable, leading to the natural inclination to obtain them (no memetic or compulsory effects have been observed).

is pure gold.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This could benefit from a rewrite. Presently, there is too much random anomalous things thrown in and slapped on everything. Ancient civilization? Check. Biological hazard? Check. Sapience? Check. Memetic effects? Check.

Good SCP? No. - 1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This honestly feels like a worse SCP-1100 with a poorly-executed message about human greed, and the exploration log suffers heavily from the "oh no MTFs go there and then THEY DIE!!!1!!!1!" cliche. -1

item.title: My first downvote!
item.content:encoded:
While individual aspects are okayish, if generic, the total is less than the sum of its parts.

Why is it black goo? Why does it disappear if prevented from reaching topsoil? What form of disappearing are we talking about? If it's intangible, how DO we stop it from reaching topsoil? What about using a spectrometer? How does land get purified from it if the perimiter receded at one point?

The treasure and mutation logs could'a would'a should'a been their own documents - they are potentially interesting, in a Deathworld kind of way, but not strictly necessary for the main article.

But the biggest thing against it is how dreadfully generic everything about it is, like a set piece from a 1990s Saturday Morning Cartoon about superheroes with an anvilicious moral lesson at the end of the episode.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is still one of my favorites from Series II. I've got some ideas if it ever comes up for a rewrite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reviewing this, as it was my first work on the site, I'm honestly surprised that it's survived, as, objectively, I don't think it's very good. I did do a minor edit to fix some of the mistakes I noticed and a few of the things people were complaining about, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is competently written, and good use of pictures, but seriously, seriously dull and generic. I've seen this a thousand times.

I'm somewhat amused that it has common errors like 'amnesiac' and titanium: the strongest metal!!!! — and has survived anyway, but hey, otherwise it's well-written.

Also, a question: Why is it always friggin' black goop? Always with the black goop.

Kind of a shame, because I wanted to like this; it's got elements I tend to enjoy such as pyramids, south american cultures, scary doofy underworlds, eldritch terraforming… but they're all executed in the most generic of ways.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like how it creates monstrous plants and animals, and overall this seemed like a solid article. Upvoted.

My one problem with this is the spot of titanium abuse, but that one little thing doesn't bother me too much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, they added titanium because it was getting stronger (even though titanium isn't all that strong). But then the thing breached containment using acid (to which titanium is resistant). A new twist on SCP-022-J?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was wondering why this had so few upvotes, then I read the discussion. Still, should have rebounded from that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Removed some redaction

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Redaction is way too common, in many places it's obviously black-boxed because you didn't want to come up with details (such as the dimensions of the structure). Black-boxes in the containment are a personal pet-peeve, even when they make half-sense like anonymizing an individual. Just remove references to Dr. BB as an individual and call him "the director of site 1888" or some such. Beyond my personal tastes, the redaction kills the tension. You just can't get woried about something that you know absolutely nothing about.
"The blanks blanked, and they have blanks instead of blanks, with a tendency to blank your blank while blanking a blank." See, it comes off as funny, not creepy.

No vote, because I like the idea, but you need to fill in the blanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoting for potential; I'd honestly say to just cut out the testing logs, this is one of those times where it's just a more explicit listing of the effects that happened in the article. As it's not one of those where the logs make it great (261 and 914 being the impossible-to-reach-standards) they don't really seem to add much beyond length in my opinion. Other grievances of mine have been repeatedly brought up by others.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added a section at the end to explain the reclassification

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting concept, but a lot of the redactions made very little sense to me. Also, I'm confused about the reclassification.

No vote for now, but I could +1 with sufficient revisions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Made some edits to the transmission log

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not a bad collection of effects, to be perfectly honest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I pretty much agree with what people above have said, but it still earns an upvote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hate to jump on the "too much redaction" bandwagon, but it's true.

Very interesting concept, and with Khym Chanur's above interpretation, I liked it even more. I think it would work better with some lightened-up redaction, and perhaps a better explanation to the whole "this was keter and now safe, and it was growing bigger but now smaller" thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the main idea of spreading the area of influence via people's greed (although apparently it can increase its influence without that, which removes some of the interest.), but there's too much redaction.

"prolonged sounds of screaming, decaying into sound of a 76 member polka band performing a cover of Daft Punk's Harder Better Faster Stronger."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree. The Foundation isn't so prudish, I feel, to remove expletives at the cost of readability.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You got my upvote with the Cold Fusion reactor and the attempt to dismantle it. I think it's a solid idea, and well constructed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a cool idea, but it needs some polishing. A tentative +1, with hopes of improvement.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hate redacted cussing, and the blackout redactions are heavy handed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I couldn't find a better place for it that wouldn't ruin the formatting

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seeing as everything was deliberately connected, what should be removed? I can see the point of too much, but what could be extracted without killing the premise of the article?

I quite enjoyed it, but I'm a little shocked to see it was posted with that picture pushed down on the side still.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Jester-level bad.

Whoa whoa whoa. Let's not say things we can't take back.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, lots of that crazy Amazon stuff looks a little fake, but this is definitely a real thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I stand corrected. Still looks fakey, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This made me upvote it. I really like the concept, personally, given this reading. I think some things need to be cleaned up n stuff. I can't think of anything offhand, but if you want, posterguy, I could read through it and give you a more detailed analysis or whatever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's a real image of a real plant.

Let me do it for you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Couldn't have put it better myself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is too much stuff crammed into one article. You've got the treasure room with a Kallisti apple in it. You've got the Darkspawn blight from Dragon Age. You've got an ancient prophecy that (like every ancient prophecy ever) turns out to be true, buried chambers of cock-blocking redactedness, and your article has been infected with "All field agents are foulmouthed sailors" syndrome with a secondary infection of "The Foundation is too prudish to commend the word 'fuck' to posterity" syndrome.

Also, the obvious CGI plant monster in the last pic is just bad. Jester-level bad.

Pick one idea and focus on it, because right now this is just all over the board. Downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What I got out of it was that the entity trapped under the pyramid is trying to "teraform" the land around the pyramid, and that it does so by means of the black gunk. But it can't move the black gunk itself, so it has to disguise the black gunk as treasures to get people to carry it out of the pyramid. So the treasure, gunk and evil creatures/plants is all tied together, rather than just being random stuff thrown together.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I still think you could use a picture of an actual pyramid. Other than that, it doesn't do anything for me. A pyramid with a treasure room. When you take the treasure, it then turns to goo. Just….meh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Removed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reclassified as Safe by authority of Dr. ██████

Remove this. It makes it look like there's gonna be a twist at the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, we've got an Aztec temple that creates cursed treasure, and you steal the treasure it turns into black gunk that makes animals and plants turn evil and try to kill you. Oh, and if you dig too deep everyone dies and/or goes crazy.

Color me unimpressed.

-1
